notes: Diamond & Pearl era set (DP2, 2007). Pack = 10 cards (the DP era moved boosters from 9 to 10 cards/pack, per Bulbapedia's Diamond & Pearl TCG page): 1 rare-or-better slot, 3 uncommon, 5 common, 1 reverse holo. Box = 36 packs, no ETB (era predates ETBs). RARE SLOT: holo ~1 in 3 packs (~12 holos/box per Flipside & HoloHaven) so within the non-Lv.X/non-secret portion ~1/3 rare-holo, ~2/3 rare. Lv.X rate 1:36 packs (Flipside Part Two; community consensus = one Lv.X per booster box for Mysterious Treasures) -> 0.0278/pack. Secret rare Time-Space Distortion (124/123) 1:72 packs = 1 per 2 boxes (Flipside Part Two) -> 0.0139/pack. Lv.X and secret-rare occupy the rare slot when hit. REVERSE HOLO slot maps onto existing buckets only (no dedicated reverse bucket): split common/uncommon/rare proportional to card counts in reverse-eligible pool (42 common, 37 uncommon, 23 rare = 102 -> 0.412/0.363/0.225). UNCOMMON and COMMON slots map 1:1 to their buckets. Confidence LOW: no per-set pull study exists; rates are DP-era averages (1:36 Lv.X, 1:72 secret) applied to this set from published community/blog sources, not a large sample study. Bulbapedia counts (18 rare-holo, 3 Lv.X, 1 secret) match the provided buckets. Vintage sealed box carries large collector premium so EV/pack will sit well below box price — expected, not a rate problem.
